description:
Goal: publish the exact Compiler2-native native-codegen contract before more code moves. Signal: docs and tests name the facts that native codegen may consume after `NativeProgram(root)` and the old planner/codegen facts that are no longer part of that contract.

Change:
- document the Compiler2-native codegen boundary in the northstar and agent docs
- map the current old-world codegen inputs to the Compiler2 facts that replace them, or record the missing native fact if one is genuinely absent
- restate `fz-rh2.8.2.3.3` in terms of the post-refactor consumer switch we actually want

Why:
- without a precise contract, the next refactor will drift into reconstructing old planner state under new names

Checks:
- docs cite the real current code paths and artifacts
- tests/notes capture which native-codegen questions are legal after `NativeProgram(root)` and which are fatal
title: Compiler2 native backends: publish the planner-free codegen contract
